Sports Scene

Copied

A delegation of Chinese mainland athletes returning from the Paris Olympics concluded their three-day visit to south China's Macao Special Administrative Region (SAR) on Monday. The delegation consisted of 65 athletes including 59 gold medalists, in addition to coaches and team leaders, who had also previously visited the Hong Kong SAR for three days.

During their visit, the Olympians held dialogues with young athletes and played matches with Macao athletes in meet-and-greet events. They also visited iconic landmarks and participated in gala events where they took stage to exhibit athletic skills and perform in front of enthusiastic crowds.

It has become a tradition for the mainland medalists to visit Hong Kong and Macao after the Olympic Games in order to foster a sense of national pride and reinforce bonds between people. Athlete representatives said they felt warmly welcomed, and that they hope to inspire younger generations with their understanding of sportsmanship.

"We received such a warm welcome and appreciation from the residents of Hong Kong SAR and Macao SAR. I didn't expect that we would attract this much attention. Your enthusiasm makes me feel like I'm coming home. I'd like to return here again," said swimmer Zhang Yufei, who won one silver and five bronze medals in Paris.

"These past few dyas, I was truly moved by your warmth and passion for sports. I hope we can inspire the younger generation through exchanges, and that they continue to be inspired by Chinese sportsmanship," said Li Yuehong, who won his maiden Olympic title in the men's 25m rapid fire pistol event.
